title: Amorphophallus mossambicensis
intro: Amorphophallus mossambicensis, also known as the Mozambique titan arum, is a species of flowering plant in the arum family. This plant has been traditionally used in African medicine, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, pain, and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Amorphophallus mossambicensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to address various health concerns, particularly those related to pain management and fever reduction.
taxonomy: Amorphophallus mossambicensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Arum. It is native to Mozambique. This plant is classified in the genus Amorphophallus. The genus Amorphophallus is part of the Arum family (Araceae). This classification makes Amorphophallus mossambicensis a member of the order Alismatales.
